institution: University of the West of England (UWE)
role: Applied Science Communication: Online and Media Writing
qualification: Applied Science Communication: Online and Media Writing
summary: Completed a Masters-level science communication module focused on writing for non-academic audiences, with training in online science writing, audience adaptation, narrative structure, risk and statistics communication, press release writing, and making science content more visible online. The assessed portfolio encouraged flexible writing across multiple genres and audiences, aligned to real-world science communication practice.
keyActivities: Identified research stories and translated them into clear public-facing content.
Adapted writing style, structure, and tone for different audiences and formats.
Produced science writing in formats such as blog posts, features, news stories, and press releases.
Applied best practice in online science communication and audience engagement.
Evaluated how to communicate scientific uncertainty, risk, and statistics effectively.
achievements: Strengthened practical skills in digital-first science communication.

role: CIM Level 4 Certificate in Professional Digital Marketing
qualification: CIM Level 4 Certificate in Professional Digital Marketing
summary: Completed a professionally recognised digital marketing qualification covering marketing impact, planning integrated campaigns, content marketing, and social media marketing. Built practical capability in campaign planning, channel strategy, audience targeting, and performance-led digital communication.
keyActivities: Studied core digital marketing concepts and campaign planning methods.
Applied content marketing and social media marketing principles.
Developed audience-focused messaging and campaign thinking.
Used analytical judgement to assess marketing performance and impact.
achievements: Achieved the CIM Level 4 Certificate in Professional Digital Marketing.
Built a strong foundation in modern digital and content marketing practice.
certifications: CIM Level 4 Certificate in Professional Digital Marketing

summary: Completed an intensive interdisciplinary space studies programme focused on space applications, systems thinking, and collaborative problem solving. Contributed to the AMBIEnT project on affordable microsatellite-based internet access and environmental monitoring, strengthening understanding of space-sector innovation, technical feasibility, and mission-level collaboration.
keyActivities: Worked on the AMBIEnT project: Affordable Microsatellite-Based Internet Access and Environmental Monitoring.
Applied systems thinking to space applications and mission concepts.
Collaborated across disciplines in an international project environment.
Evaluated the technical and societal value of proposed space services.
achievements: Contributed to an interdisciplinary project exploring microsatellite applications.
Built experience in international collaboration on a space-sector concept.
projects: AMBIEnT - Affordable Microsatellite-Based Internet Access and Environmental Monitoring

summary: Completed an integrated master's degree in physics with astrophysics, combining core physics, mathematics, and specialist astronomy study. Developed strong analytical, mathematical, and scientific computing skills through rigorous quantitative training and research preparation.
keyActivities: Studied core physics topics alongside specialist astrophysics modules.
Applied mathematics to scientific modelling and problem solving.
Developed scientific computing and data analysis skills.
Strengthened understanding of physical systems through advanced quantitative study.
achievements: Completed an MPhys degree with astrophysics specialism.
Built a strong academic foundation in physics, astronomy, and scientific analysis.

summary: Conducted doctoral research on Jupiter-family comets within the SEPPCoN collaboration, analysing optical and thermal infrared observations from major observatories including VLT, Palomar, Keck, and Spitzer. Investigated comet size, spin, albedo, thermal behaviour, and composition, contributing to scientific understanding of small-body populations and observational astronomy.
keyActivities: Analysed astronomical datasets from optical and thermal infrared observations.
Processed and interpreted comet imagery and observational data.
Conducted observational astronomy and thermal infrared analysis.
Collaborated with researchers in the SEPPCoN project.
Contributed to research into Jupiter-family comet physical properties.
achievements: Contributed to research on the physical properties and size distribution of Jupiter-family cometary nuclei.
Worked with data from major observatories and space-based infrared facilities.
Supported scientific understanding of cometary populations relevant to planetary science.
